text:
The purpose of this research is to develop a theory base for the concept of the “quantum law” as a futuristic legal model. There is an essential need for a new regulatory framework dealing with quantum phenomena and the associated risks as well as dangers emerging in the course of the quantum technology implementation. An analysis of the existing legislation for Russia and foreign countries has shown that it is vital to establish a legal framework for the development of quantum technologies.

The authors used a multidisciplinary approach by integrating systems analysis with various techniques and methods from engineering and law. They used a comparative-legal method for mapping the development of quantum technology regulation in different countries and legal models for designing the structure of quantum law, including its underlying ethical and legal norms.

The study explores the consequences of quantum technologies, which can fundamentally change and are developing at a rate that current legislation is not capable of regulating and even pose a number of global risks. The study makes a systematic analysis of current regulatory practices in various countries and outlines the main lines of development of the Russian legislation in this area, which allows to summarize the main concepts of quantum law and to outline the main vectors of its further development.

This article is the first in legal scholarship to consider national and international quantum regulation and thereby make a case for the emergence of a new branch of law — quantum law. In practical terms, the article provides a roadmap for any future quantum legislation and enforcement activities and thus equips lawmakers and other researchers with the legal theories they will need to cope with the many new challenges that the quantum age is bound to bring.
